title: unona sempervivens
intro: Unona sempervivens, also known as Senna, is a plant species that belongs to the legume family and is native to tropical Africa. Traditionally, Unona sempervivens has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its anthelmintic, anti-inflammatory, and purgative effects. The plant's health benefits include treating constipation, reducing fever, and alleviating symptoms of various gastrointestinal disorders. Unona sempervivens is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be prepared by boiling the plant's leaves and roots in water or mixing them with other herbs in a solvent.
